Client tracker preview for Connected Birth

This page shows how the embedded screenshots fit together as a working Notion setup for Connected Birth: A single tracker for Christy's Vermont home birth practice — consults, prenatal visits, on-call window, home birth, and the six-week postpartum arc in one place instead of scattered across her inbox and phone.

What this is

A preview of a duplicate-ready Notion client tracker organized around the public workflow visible on the site.

Public workflow used

Compassionate Vermont home birth and midwifery care in Williston and surrounding communities, What's your birth story and what are your dreams for this pregnancy and birth?, Seamless transfer coordination with local hospitals if necessary, Neonatal assessment and newborn screenings after birth

Today at Connected Birth Prenatal + birth + postpartum board Heloise P. Record Prenatal + Lab View Prenatal cadence + on-call window Postpartum visit + newborn care view Setup Map

Why this shape

From overview to usable workspace

The first screenshots show the main views. The later screenshots show the editable structure that would be delivered: fields, focused tables, individual client pages, and starter records.

What it helps with

  • Every new family's contact-form message lands in one queue with consult status, instead of in Christy's email alongside everything else
  • Prenatal cadence (monthly, then every-two-weeks, then weekly), labs and anatomy scan, and the 37-42 week on-call window visible on one timeline
  • Six-week postpartum + newborn screenings arc (24h, 1wk, 6wk) tracked in one place — no paper chart to rebuild after each visit

1. Today at Connected Birth

Today at Connected Birth

Families in care, prenatals this week, who's in the on-call window, and which postpartum visits are due — one snapshot instead of flipping between phone, email, and paper chart.

Open this view

2. Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Prenatal + birth + postpartum board

Every family moves left-to-right: Inquiry -> Free consult -> Intake + history -> Prenatal cadence -> On-call window (37-42 wks) -> Postpartum arc -> Newborn screenings. Contact-form messages land in one queue.

Open this view

3. Heloise P. Record

Heloise P. Record

Per-family page: EDD, intake + medical history, prenatal cadence, labs and scans, homebirth logistics (birth kit, home prep, backup plan), on-call window, and the six-week postpartum plan.

Open this view

4. Prenatal + Lab View

Prenatal + Lab View

One screen for the current prenatal: gestational age, vitals, fundal/fetal heart, lab results waiting for follow-up (glucose, GBS, CBC), and the next step — so labs don't slip between visits.

Open this view

5. Prenatal cadence + on-call window

Prenatal cadence + on-call window

This week's prenatals, every active family's on-call window (37-42 wks) with birth-kit and backup-transfer status, labs/scans due, and apprentice-coverage windows — all on one timeline.

Open this view

6. Postpartum visit + newborn care view

Postpartum visit + newborn care view

After every birth: the 24h / 3-day / 1-wk / 2-wk / 6-wk visit cadence with newborn exam, PKU/metabolic screen, hearing screen coordination, and lactation check-ins — so no postpartum family falls off the list.

Open this view

7. Setup Map

Setup Map

Six-step setup: duplicate the template, move contact-form messages into the Inquiry queue, paste intake + history per family, set prenatal cadence reminders, mark each on-call window, and schedule postpartum visit cadence at the birth.

Open this view
